reasoning: The resolution requires the Philippines to experience over 10 times its 30-day average of fatalities (which currently sets a target of ~496 fatalities in a 30-day window). Historically, even major outbreaks like the Marawi siege (~240 fatalities/month on average) or peak periods of the Drug War did not reach this 500-fatality threshold in a single 30-day ACLED period. The current political climate features tensions between the Marcos and Duterte camps, and Duterte was recently arrested, but the Armed Forces of the Philippines have strongly denied any military unrest, making a sudden eruption of a 500-fatality civil war highly unlikely. Moreover, international tensions in the South China Sea, while elevated, remain below the threshold of hot asymmetric warfare. A 10x spike is an extraordinarily high bar that effectively requires the commencement of a massive conventional war or an unprecedented wave of systemic massacres. The probability for near-term dates (which partially or completely cover the present and recent past) is at the minimum allowed bound (0.02), increasing only slightly for further dates (0.03, 0.04) to account for increasing uncertainty and tail risks over a longer one-year forecasting horizon.
